translation.en: I want to grasp important information.

content.sentence: Tôi muốn ___ thông tin quan trọng.

exampleSentencesTranslated: He grasp your hand tightly when crossing the busy street.
To succeed at work, you need to grasp the basic knowledge.
She tries to grasp this opportunity to develop herself.

definitions.vi: Nắm chặt hoặc giữ một cái gì đó một cách chắc chắn.
definitions.en: To grasp or hold something tightly.

example.es: Sostengo mi mano firmemente para no caer.
example.vi: Tôi nắm chặt tay để không bị ngã.
example.en: I hold my hand tightly to avoid falling.

notes:
nắm = to grasp or hold with your hand. Very often, it also means to grasp/understand or to have control of something.

It’s a verb: you nắm + noun. Common collocations: nắm tay (hold hands), nắm chặt (hold tightly), nắm rõ / nắm vững (understand well), nắm thông tin (have information), nắm quyền (hold power).

Physically, it’s close to “hold” (giữ). Figuratively, it’s close to “understand” (hiểu), but with a stronger sense of firm control.
exampleSentences: Anh nắm tay em thật chặt khi băng qua đường đông.
Để thành công trong công việc, bạn cần nắm kiến thức cơ bản.
Chị cố gắng nắm cơ hội này để phát triển bản thân.

translation.en: I want to grasp important information.

content.sentence: Tôi muốn ___ thông tin quan trọng.

notes_md:
**nắm** = to grasp or hold with your hand. Very often, it also means to grasp/understand or to have control of something.

It’s a verb: you **nắm + noun**. Common collocations: *nắm tay* (hold hands), *nắm chặt* (hold tightly), *nắm rõ / nắm vững* (understand well), *nắm thông tin* (have information), *nắm quyền* (hold power).

Physically, it’s close to “hold” (*giữ*). Figuratively, it’s close to “understand” (*hiểu*), but with a stronger sense of firm control.
